title: dactyloctenium hackelii
intro: Dactyloctenium hackelii, also known as finger grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Dactyloctenium hackelii has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help in treating various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, Dactyloctenium hackelii is often used as a tea or decoction, and its leaves can also be used to make a poultice or infused oil for topical application.
